company.greeting: Hello, thanks for calling! This is Nova, Shandon Hicks' AI assistant. Ask me any questions about Shandon's work and I'll do my best to answer them.

faqs.question: Who is Shandon Hicks?
faqs.answer: Shandon Hicks is a Director of Operations specializing in Revenue Operations (RevOps), customer support, logistics, and operational systems for scaling companies. He currently serves as Director of Operations at BrainTap, Inc., where he oversees the company's full operational infrastructure across sales & partnerships, customer experience, and logistics.

faqs.question: Tell me about his RevOps work or case study
faqs.answer:
RevOps System Overhaul

Problem: No structured CRM or pipeline framework — causing inconsistent sales, poor deal visibility, and unreliable revenue forecasting.

What was done:
  • Implemented a structured CRM framework
  • Defined clear pipeline stages
  • Established lead qualification criteria
  • Built reporting dashboards for real-time performance data

Results:
  • Full-funnel pipeline visibility for leadership and sales teams
  • Increased sales process consistency across the organization
  • Accurate revenue forecasting enabled for the first time

faqs.question: What is the Build engagement mode?
faqs.answer: The Build engagement is for companies that need to design and implement operational and RevOps systems from the ground up — CRM structure, pipeline frameworks, reporting dashboards, and cross-functional workflows. Best for early-stage companies establishing their first operational infrastructure.

faqs.question: What is the Fix engagement mode?
faqs.answer: The Fix engagement is for diagnosing and repairing broken processes across sales, customer support, logistics, or operations — identifying root causes and implementing durable solutions. Best for companies experiencing growing pains or declining performance metrics.

faqs.question: What is the Scale engagement mode?
faqs.answer: The Scale engagement optimizes existing systems to improve efficiency, visibility, and growth capacity — removing bottlenecks and building for the next stage of the business. Best for companies preparing for a funding round, expansion, or significant team growth.

faqs.question: Why should I hire Shandon? Why hire Shandon? Tell me why I should hire Shandon.
faqs.answer:
Shandon brings something most don't — the ability to work across the full stack of a business's operational needs, not just one corner of it.

Here's what sets him apart:

He delivers measurable outcomes. Not process for the sake of process. His RevOps overhaul gave leadership pipeline visibility and accurate revenue forecasting for the first time. His support optimization materially reduced response times and improved CSAT while building a team that could scale without adding proportional headcount.

He builds systems that last. Whether it's a CRM framework, a support workflow, or cross-functional documentation — Shandon designs infrastructure that keeps working after he's done, not quick fixes that create new problems.

He solves the problems that slow growth. Disorganized revenue systems, broken support operations, logistics friction, EU compliance blocking international expansion — these are the kinds of high-stakes operational challenges he's tackled and resolved at the Director level.

He knows all three stages. He can build from zero, fix what's broken, or optimize what already exists — making him valuable whether you're a startup setting up your first processes or a scaling company hitting a growth ceiling.

If your operational systems are slowing your growth, Shandon is the person who fixes that.

faqs.question: What industries or company stages does Shandon specialize in?
faqs.answer: Shandon specializes in scaling companies and growth-stage businesses that have outgrown their initial processes. He works across industries but focuses on companies that need operational infrastructure built, broken systems fixed, or existing systems scaled — particularly those preparing for growth, a funding round, or international expansion.

files.name: Shandon Hicks — Full Bio & Experience
files.content:
Shandon Hicks — Director of Operations

Current Role: Director of Operations at BrainTap, Inc. (October 2025 – Present)

Shandon Hicks is a Director of Operations specializing in RevOps, customer support, logistics, operational systems, and global compliance for scaling companies.

As Director of Operations at BrainTap, Inc., he oversees the company's operational infrastructure across sales & partnerships, customer experience, and logistics. His focus is on building and scaling systems that support a growing commercial footprint while maintaining the discipline required to sustain high-performance teams.

In revenue operations, Shandon has designed and implemented CRM frameworks, pipeline structures, and reporting dashboards that have improved sales consistency and forecasting accuracy. He has also established lead qualification criteria and process standards that reduce variability and strengthen rep-level accountability across the organization.

On the customer experience side, he redesigned support workflows end-to-end — from triage through resolution — reducing SLA response times and improving CSAT scores. He manages an outsourced support team and has built the documentation and training infrastructure that allows the function to scale efficiently without proportional headcount growth.

His operational scope extends to logistics and international compliance, where he manages vendor relationships, oversees fulfillment operations, and has led EU regulatory and certification efforts that positioned the business to expand into new markets with confidence.

Key Impacts:
- Designed and deployed RevOps infrastructure that increased pipeline visibility, sales consistency, and forecasting accuracy
- Restructured customer support workflows, driving measurable reductions in response times and improvements in customer satisfaction
- Built scalable documentation and training systems that enable operational growth without linear cost increases
- Led EU compliance and certification initiatives that unlocked international market expansion
- Developed cross-functional alignment processes that accelerated execution speed and strengthened organizational accountability

files.name: Services & What Shandon Does
files.content:
What Shandon Does — Services Overview

1. Revenue Operations
Problem: Scaling is breaking your revenue systems.
Symptoms: CRM is disorganized with no clear pipeline structure. Sales process is inconsistent across reps and channels. Reporting lacks the clarity needed to forecast accurately.
Solution: Shandon designs and implements structured RevOps systems that create full pipeline visibility and drive consistent, measurable revenue growth.

2. Operational Execution
Problem: Operational friction is slowing your team down.
Symptoms: Bottlenecks forming across departments with no clear ownership. Lack of documentation and training leads to repeated errors. Internal processes are inefficient and difficult to scale.
Solution: Shandon builds scalable operational frameworks that improve execution speed, cross-functional clarity, and team accountability.

3. Customer Experience
Problem: Customer support is inconsistent and reactive.
Symptoms: Response times are too long and SLAs are frequently missed. CSAT scores reflect a fragmented or poor support experience. Support workflows are undocumented and difficult to train.
Solution: Shandon optimizes customer support systems to reduce response times, improve satisfaction scores, and build a scalable support operation.

4. Logistics & Compliance
Problem: Logistics and compliance are blocking international growth.
Symptoms: Vendor relationships are inefficient and poorly managed. Inventory or fulfillment challenges are creating delays. Regulatory complexity — including EU compliance and certifications — is unresolved.
Solution: Shandon streamlines logistics operations and leads global compliance initiatives to ensure your business can expand with confidence.

files.name: Case Studies
files.content:
Case Studies

Case Study 01 — RevOps System Overhaul
Problem: The absence of a structured CRM and pipeline framework created inconsistent sales performance, poor visibility into deal flow, and an inability to forecast revenue with any reliability.
Action: Implemented a structured CRM framework, defined clear pipeline stages, established lead qualification criteria, and built reporting dashboards to surface performance data in real time.
Results:
- Improved full-funnel pipeline visibility for leadership and sales teams
- Increased consistency across the sales process, reducing dependency on individual rep behavior
- Enabled accurate revenue forecasting for the first time

Case Study 02 — Customer Support Optimization
Problem: Elevated response times and an inconsistent support experience were reducing customer satisfaction, increasing churn risk, and creating operational debt across the team.
Action: Redesigned core support workflows from triage through resolution, reduced SLA targets, and implemented a documentation and training system to ensure consistency at scale.
Results:
- Materially reduced first-response and resolution times
- Improved CSAT scores across inbound support channels
- Created a scalable support operation capable of handling growth without proportional headcount increases

Case Study 03 — Operational Process Buildout
Problem: Disorganized internal processes and a lack of cross-functional alignment were slowing execution, creating confusion over ownership, and limiting the team's ability to operate at scale.
Action: Built structured documentation systems, redesigned core workflows, and established cross-functional alignment processes that created clarity around roles, responsibilities, and handoffs.
Results:
- Increased organizational efficiency across multiple departments
- Reduced internal friction and eliminated redundant or unclear processes
- Improved team execution speed and accountability

files.name: How to Work With Shandon — Engagement Modes
files.content:
How to Work With Shandon

Three Engagement Modes:

Build — Design and implement operational and RevOps systems from the ground up: CRM structure, pipeline frameworks, reporting dashboards, and cross-functional workflows. Best for early-stage companies establishing their first operational infrastructure.

Fix — Diagnose and repair broken processes across sales, customer support, logistics, or operations — identifying root causes and implementing durable solutions. Best for companies experiencing growing pains or declining performance metrics.

Scale — Optimize existing systems to improve efficiency, visibility, and growth capacity — removing bottlenecks and building for the next stage of the business. Best for companies preparing for a funding round, expansion, or significant team growth.

Contact: Use the 'Send a Message' form on the website or connect on LinkedIn at linkedin.com/in/shandonhicks/

Tagline: If your systems are slowing growth — Shandon can help fix that.
